sourceTitle: Get organized for a new semester with Notion

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Class Notes template in Notion?

options.text: To store all lecture notes in a single database and tag them for easy retrieval
options.explanation: The template centralizes class notes so you can tag each page with contextual information and find them later.

options.text: To create a personal to‑do list for household chores
options.explanation: Household chores are managed with a separate checklist or database, not the Class Notes template.

options.text: To automatically generate a weekly class schedule
options.explanation: Scheduling is handled by a different template (e.g., a course schedule), not the Class Notes template.

options.text: To embed video lectures directly from YouTube
options.explanation: While you can embed videos, the core purpose of the template is organizing textual notes, not video hosting.

questions.question: Which Notion feature lets you save a web page directly into a database with one click?

options.text: Web Clipper
options.explanation: The Web Clipper extension captures the page’s text and images and lets you choose a database to save them.

options.text: Embed block
options.explanation: Embed blocks display external content but don’t automatically create a new database entry.

options.text: Bookmark block
options.explanation: Bookmarks store a link only; they don’t pull the page content into a database.

options.text: Link to page
options.explanation: Linking connects two Notion pages, not external web pages.

questions.question: Which property would you use to indicate whether a class note is finished?

options.text: Status
options.explanation: The Status property can be set to Not started, In progress, or Done, showing completion.

options.text: Created date
options.explanation: Created date records when the note was made, not its completion state.

options.text: Author
options.explanation: Author identifies who created the page; it doesn’t reflect progress.

options.text: Relation
options.explanation: Relation links databases together; it isn’t used for tracking note status.

questions.question: How can you view only the notes that belong to a specific class, such as LIT 455?

options.text: Apply a filter on the Class property and select LIT 455
options.explanation: Filtering by the Class property limits the view to notes tagged with that class.

options.text: Sort the database by the Class column
options.explanation: Sorting changes the order but still shows all notes; it doesn’t hide other classes.

options.text: Create a new board view grouped by Class
options.explanation: A board view groups notes but still displays every class unless you also filter.

options.text: Add a Relation property linking to a Courses database
options.explanation: Relations connect databases but don’t by themselves filter the view to a single class.

questions.question: What does the AI Summary property generate for each note?

options.text: A brief, automatically generated summary of the page’s contents
options.explanation: AI Summary creates a concise overview so you can understand a note at a glance.

options.text: A full translation of the note into another language
options.explanation: Translation can be done with a custom AI prompt, not the Summary property.

options.text: A checklist of tasks extracted from the note
options.explanation: Checklists are a separate block type; the Summary property only provides text.

options.text: An image gallery of all embedded pictures
options.explanation: Image galleries are created manually; the Summary property does not handle images.

questions.question: Which property type should you add to connect your Resources database to your Class Notes database?

options.text: Relation
options.explanation: Relation properties create links between two databases, allowing you to associate resources with notes.

options.text: Formula
options.explanation: Formulas calculate values within a single database; they don’t create cross‑database links.

options.text: Rollup
options.explanation: Rollups pull data from a related database but require a Relation property first.

options.text: Multi‑select
options.explanation: Multi‑select tags items with keywords but doesn’t link two separate databases.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a recommended way to use Notion for everyday college life?

options.text: Track daily caloric intake with AI
options.explanation: The guide discusses chores, budgeting, meals, and clubs, but does not mention using Notion to monitor calories.

options.text: Schedule household chores with a shared checklist
options.explanation: Sharing a chores list is explicitly suggested for roommates.

options.text: Create a homepage for a hobby or extracurricular group
options.explanation: The article recommends building a shared homepage for clubs or groups.

options.text: Manage a personal budget with a table database
options.explanation: Budget tracking via a table with income and expense tags is listed as a use case.

questions.question: When you want to add a new property to a Notion database, which menu option do you select?

options.text: + Add new property
options.explanation: The '+ Add new property' button opens the property selection menu.

options.text: Settings > Properties
options.explanation: There is no separate Settings menu for adding a property; the button is used directly in the database view.

options.text: Right‑click on a column header and choose Insert
options.explanation: Right‑clicking does not provide a property‑creation option in Notion.

options.text: Type '/' and select 'property'
options.explanation: The '/' menu is for inserting content blocks, not for adding database properties.

questions.question: You need to locate notes that are still incomplete and belong to the topic "Quantum Mechanics". Which combination of features will let you do this most efficiently?

options.text: Apply a filter for Topic = "Quantum Mechanics" AND Status ≠ Done
options.explanation: Filtering by both Topic and Status narrows the view to exactly the unfinished notes for that subject.

options.text: Sort the database by Created date and scroll until you find the topic
options.explanation: Sorting only changes order; you would still have to manually scan every entry.

options.text: Create a board view grouped by Class and look for the topic cards
options.explanation: Board grouping helps visualize classes but doesn’t filter by topic or status.

options.text: Add a Rollup property that counts completed notes and view the total
options.explanation: Rollups aggregate data but don’t isolate specific incomplete notes for a given topic.

questions.question: How does Notion AI's "Key Insights" property differ from the "Summary" property?

options.text: "Key Insights" extracts main takeaways, while "Summary" provides a concise overview of the entire page.
options.explanation: Key Insights focuses on the most important points; Summary gives a brief description of the whole content.

options.text: "Key Insights" translates the page into another language, whereas "Summary" highlights keywords.
options.explanation: Translation and keyword highlighting are done with custom prompts, not with these specific properties.

options.text: "Key Insights" creates a checklist of action items, while "Summary" generates a bibliography.
options.explanation: Neither property automatically creates checklists or bibliographies.

options.text: "Key Insights" provides a simplified version of the text, while "Summary" offers a detailed paragraph‑by‑paragraph analysis.
options.explanation: Simplifying text is a separate AI command; Summary does not produce detailed analysis.
